Generate_script_prompt:
Prompt: Give a quick tech news update script in the style of CNBC techcheck briefing as an example.
        Response: I'm Wall-E, and this is your CNBC techcheck Briefing for Monday April 29th. Tesla is asking shareholders to reinstate CEO Elon Musk's $56 billion pay package, which a Delaware judge voided earlier this year. The judge ruled that the record-setting compensation deal was, quote, deeply flawed. Tesla also saying it would ask shareholders to approve moving the company's incorporation from Delaware to Texas. The company has hired a proxy solicitor and plans to spend millions of dollars to help secure votes for the two proposals. Apple CEO Tim Cook says the company plans to look at manufacturing in Indonesia following a meeting with the country's president, Cook telling reporters following the meeting that he spoke with the president about his desire to see manufacturing there and that he believes in the country. The comments come as Apple is pushed to diversify its supply chain with more manufacturing outside of China in countries such as Vietnam and India. Shares of ASML falling today as the company missed its sales forecast but stuck to its full-year outlook. Net sales fell over 21 percent year-over-year, while net income dropped over 37 percent. ASML is highly important to the semiconductor industry as it builds machines that are required for manufacturing chips globally. Last year, weaker demand for consumer electronics hit chipmakers that produce for those devices, which has in turn impacted ASML. That's all for today. We'll see you back here tomorrow.
        Prompt: Give a quick tech news update script in the style of CNBC techcheck briefing using the following news titles and content. Response:
Script:
i'm wall-e, and this is your ai briefing for thursday, april 16th. 

amazon is making waves as it unveils its latest generative ai-powered shopping experience, promising to revolutionize how customers discover products. the company highlights that this innovative feature will allow users to receive personalized recommendations and shopping lists curated by ai, offering a seamless and tailored shopping journey. with the rise of ai technology, amazon is pushing boundaries in enhancing customer experience and engagement. 

over in the world of social media, tiktok is doubling down on its e-commerce ambitions by launching a storefront feature for creators and brands. this move positions tiktok strongly against competitors like instagram and youtube, leveraging its massive user base to explore new monetization avenues. the storefronts will allow creators to showcase products directly in their profiles, enhancing commercial opportunities within the app. 

meanwhile, shares of nvidia have surged yet again after the chipmaker announced its latest advancements in ai processing technology. the company revealed that its new line of gpus, designed to accelerate ai model training, will hit the market later this quarter. nvidia's ceo stressed that these innovations are poised to set a new standard for computational performance and efficiency, amid heightened demand for ai applications.

that's all for today. we'll see you back here tomorrow
Polished Script:
i'm wall-e, welcoming you to today's tech briefing for thursday, april 16th. 

amazon is making headlines today with the unveiling of its new generative ai-powered shopping experience, set to transform how customers discover products. this cutting-edge feature promises ai-curated personalized recommendations and shopping lists, aiming to enhance the consumer journey with greater customization. as ai technology continues to evolve, amazon is pushing the envelope to heighten customer engagement and satisfaction.

in the realm of social media, tiktok is ramping up its e-commerce efforts, introducing a storefront feature for creators and brands. this strategic move positions tiktok against heavyweights like instagram and youtube, leveraging its vast user base to explore new monetization routes. the storefronts will enable creators to promote products directly on their profiles, expanding commercial possibilities within the app.

meanwhile, nvidia shares have jumped following the announcement of their latest advancements in ai processing technology. the chipmaker revealed a new line of gpus designed to accelerate ai model training, expected to launch later this quarter. nvidia's ceo emphasized that these innovations will set a new benchmark for computational performance and efficiency, catering to the rising demand for ai applications.

that's all for today. we'll see you back here tomorrow

Podcast Description:
```html

join wall-e on thursday, april 16th, as we dive into the latest tech innovations shaping our world:

  • amazon's ai-powered shopping experience: unveiling a revolutionary shopping feature offering ai-curated personalized recommendations and shopping lists to elevate customer engagement and satisfaction.
  • tiktok's e-commerce expansion: introducing a storefront feature for creators and brands to sell products directly on their profiles, challenging major platforms like instagram and youtube in the social commerce arena.
  • nvidia's ai processing leap: announcement of a new line of gpus designed to enhance ai model training efficiency, poised to set new standards in computational performance and energy efficiency in the coming quarter.

tune in tomorrow for more tech insights and updates!

```
